energy.headline: PG&E Partners with SPAN to Help Customers Electrify Homes More Affordably
energy.summary: Pacific Gas and Electric Company announced a strategic collaboration with SPAN to deploy their SPAN Edge solution to help customers electrify their homes more affordably and efficiently.

energy.headline: FranklinWH Achieves TIA-942 Certified Battery System, Setting Global Reliability Benchmark
energy.summary: FranklinWH became the world's first residential battery system to earn TIA-942 Rated 1-4 certification, bringing enterprise-grade reliability to home energy storage.

energy.headline: SOLRITE Opens Battery-Only VPP Option for Homes Without Rooftop Solar
energy.summary: SOLRITE Energy launched a battery-only virtual power plant option enabling households without solar panels to participate in VPPs and save on utility bills.

energy.headline: California Residents Increasingly Pair Battery Storage with Solar Installations
energy.summary: Over 50% of residential solar photovoltaic installations in California were paired with battery storage in April 2024, up from just over 20% in October 2023.

energy.headline: Sacramento Municipal Utility District to Launch Major Battery Storage Project
energy.summary: SMUD will build a 160-megawatt, 640-megawatt-hour battery energy storage system on a decommissioned nuclear power plant site, with construction starting in June 2026.

energy.headline: Federal Solar Tax Credit Expires December 31, 2025; State Incentives Remain
energy.summary: The 30% federal residential clean energy tax credit for solar and battery systems ended on December 31, 2025, but state rebates and net metering programs continue to offer significant savings.

energy.headline: Battery Pack Costs Drop to $108/kWh; Further 3% Decline Expected in 2026
energy.summary: Bloomberg NEF reports battery pack costs fell to $108/kWh with projections for an additional 3% decline in 2026, enabling cost-effective multi-day backup power systems.

energy.headline: Residential Solar Prices Fall to Record Low of $2.48/W
energy.summary: Median standalone residential solar quoted prices fell to $2.48 per watt in the first half of 2025, tying the record low from late 2024, despite market headwinds.

energy.headline: Arizona's FranklinWH Supports Both SRP and APS Virtual Power Plant Programs
energy.summary: FranklinWH became the first residential energy storage provider to support both Arizona's major utility VPP programs, delivering financial incentives during rising electricity costs.

energy.headline: SEIA Reports 30% Year-Over-Year Growth in U.S. Battery Storage Market
energy.summary: The U.S. battery storage market grew 30% year-over-year despite industry bracing for supply chain restructuring and political landscape shifts.

energy.headline: Bifacial Solar Technology Moves to Mainstream in 2026 Commercial Installations
energy.summary: Bifacial technology has evolved from premium option to mainstream choice in commercial and utility installations where reflected light opportunities are plentiful.

energy.headline: Building-Integrated Photovoltaics Embedding Solar into Everyday Construction
energy.summary: BIPV technology embeds solar cells directly into building materials like roof tiles and façade panels, eliminating separate panel racks and integrating generation into structures.

energy.headline: Home Battery Storage Rental Model Emerges; Landlords Earn $5,000-$50,000 Annually
energy.summary: Property owners are increasingly renting roof space for solar and battery co-located systems, with residential properties earning $5,000-$15,000 annually and commercial buildings up to $50,000+.

energy.headline: 34 States Plus D.C. Have Mandatory Net Metering Rules in Place
energy.summary: Net metering remains available in 34 states plus Washington D.C. and Puerto Rico, with policies varying from full retail rate credits to reduced compensation models.

energy.headline: California NEM 3.0 Reduces Export Rates 75%, From 30 Cents to 8 Cents Per kWh
energy.summary: California's Net Energy Metering 3.0 policy, effective April 2023, slashed compensation for exported electricity by approximately 75%, increasing payback periods.

energy.headline: Colorado Solar Incentives Include Property Tax Exemptions and Net Metering
energy.summary: Colorado homeowners benefit from property tax exemptions, widespread net metering, and utility programs supporting residential solar despite federal credit expiration.

energy.headline: SMUD Invests $25 Million Through 2030 to Incentivize Battery Storage Adoption
energy.summary: Sacramento Municipal Utility District committed $25 million through 2030 to incentivize battery storage adoption and transition market from rooftop solar only to solar-plus-storage.

energy.headline: Puerto Rico's Solar Revolution Reaches 175,000 Residential Installations with Storage
energy.summary: Puerto Rico's residential solar market reached 175,000 homes with solar, and 160,000 of those including battery storage, following Hurricanes Irma and Maria.

energy.headline: Massachusetts Low-Income Solar Program Delivers $150/Month Energy Savings on Average
energy.summary: A pilot program providing free solar panels and heat pumps to 55 Cape Cod and Martha's Vineyard households resulted in average monthly energy savings of $150.

energy.headline: California's Net Metering Subsidy Cost Surges to $8.5 Billion by End of 2024
energy.summary: The cost of California's net metering subsidies increased from $3.4 billion in 2021 to $8.5 billion by end of 2024, affecting non-solar customers' electricity bills.

energy.headline: Virtual Power Plants Dispatched 535 MW From 100,000 California Homes During Peak Demand
energy.summary: Sunrun's virtual power plant test dispatched 535 megawatts from over 100,000 California homes on July 29, 2025, meeting half of San Francisco's evening energy demand.

energy.headline: Tallahassee Florida Battery Storage Project Valued at $39 Million with $28.7M DOE Grant
energy.summary: Tallahassee's Electric and Gas Utility announced a $39 million battery energy storage system project at Birmingham Street Substation, supported by DOE funding.

energy.headline: Arizona Adopts 'Efficiency Arizona' Program Offering Up to $8,000 for Heat Pumps
energy.summary: Arizona Governor's Office launched 'Efficiency Arizona' to administer federal HOMES and HEAR rebate funds with up to $8,000 for heat pumps and $1,750 for heat pump water heaters.

energy.headline: West Virginians Successfully Defend Against AEP's Two-Thirds Net Metering Rate Cut
energy.summary: West Virginia residents successfully fought back against AEP's proposal to slash net metering rates by two-thirds in 2025, preserving current compensation levels.
